name: Saylani Welfare Trust Usa
mission: Saylani Welfare International Trust has been working since 1999 to improve the conditions of the less privileged, helpless, and handicapped individuals ...
theoryOfChange: Combining immediate relief (food/medical) with long-term empowerment (IT training/education) to break the cycle of poverty.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2022 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.
zakatClaimEvidence: Saylani Welfare Trust - USA's donation page explicitly lists "Sadqa & Zakat" as a donation option. Additionally, the FAQ section on their donation page includes the question, "Can I pay my zakat donation or only sadqa (non-zakat) donaitons are accepted?" Furthermore, "Zakat" is listed as a "Donation type" for the Saylani Children Hospital.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 39/50, the charity shows high donor fit for those seeking Zakat-eligible humanitarian causes [3]. Its long track record since 1999 and focus on urgent human needs like hunger and healthcare contribute to its strong standing in the community [1][2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a direct service model, allocating 98.8% of its $2,954,546 total revenue directly to programs such as free meals and medical aid [1][2]. While its financial health is rated highly at 90.5/100, the impact score of 29/50 reflects a balance between high spending efficiency and a lack of specific cost-per-beneficiary data [1].

baseline_narrative.summary: Established as part of a mission starting in 1999, Saylani Welfare Trust USA focuses on improving conditions for the less privileged through medical support and free meal programs like Saylani Dastarkhwan [1][2]. The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill religious obligations through various giving methods [3].

strengths.detail: The charity directs a <cite id="1">perfect 100.0% of its expenses to programs</cite>, significantly exceeding the <cite id="1">industry standard of 75%</cite>.
The organization holds an <cite id="1">overall score of 90.90.5/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting strong governance and transparency.
Saylani maintains a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, a rare metric that maximizes donor impact.

strengths_deep_dive: Saylani Welfare Trust USA distinguishes itself through a lean operational structure that prioritizes direct service delivery. With <cite id="2">$1,727,397 in program service revenue</cite>, the organization manages to fund extensive international relief efforts, including <cite id="2">$4,469,000 in foreign grants</cite> to implementing partners. This high-volume throughput is supported by a <cite id="1">perfect score of 100 in Culture & Measures</cite> from Charity Navigator, indicating a strong internal alignment with its humanitarian mission. Furthermore, the charity holds a Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid, reinforcing its commitment to public disclosure.
rich_narrative.summary: Saylani Welfare Trust USA operates as a critical humanitarian link for vulnerable populations in Pakistan, focusing on food security, medical support, and education. The organization has demonstrated explosive growth, reporting a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 1263.3%</cite>, reaching <cite id="2">$1,727,397 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. Its operational model is characterized by extreme financial efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 98.8%</cite> according to recent filings. This efficiency is further highlighted by a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that virtually every dollar donated is directed toward its core missions, such as the <cite id="3">Saylani Dastarkhwan free meal program</cite> and specialized medical services. While the U.S. entity is relatively young, it draws on a mission legacy <cite id="3">dating back to 1999</cite>, providing a robust framework for its <cite id="3">zakat-eligible humanitarian interventions</cite>.
